title: staff — product analytics

events.name: Staff Hired
events.description: Admin hired a staff member. position_bucket is pre-computed (coaching / reception / management / support / other) to avoid high-cardinality free-text labels.

events.name: Staff Suspended
events.description: Consent tier: essential (compliance signal for discrimination-pattern detection). Never carries the suspension_reason string.

events.name: Compensation Changed
events.description: PRIVATE-TIER analytics — payload NEVER carries the amount. Only the FACT that a change happened + which fields + the compensation_type category (hourly / salary / stipend / volunteer / commission). Powers HR-compliance dashboards for pay-transparency audits. Consent tier: essential (compensation-change frequency is a discrimination-pattern signal that cannot be opted out).

events.name: Branch Manager Invariant Violated
events.description: P1 event — every fire indicates data-integrity concern. Consent tier: essential (safety + integrity).

notes: Analytics NEVER carry raw compensation_amount_cents — the Compensation Changed event uses only category + fact-of-change. Prevents accidental exposure via the analytics sink.

user_id + staff_id + coach_id are included as properties on functional-tier events for the tenant's own HR analytics. GDPR consent tier 'functional' covers first-party operational analytics per notifications::consent-tiers.json.

The 'essential' consent tier for Staff Suspended and Compensation Changed is deliberate — compliance signals (anti-discrimination pattern detection) cannot be opted out. Applies platform-side observability, not tenant-visible dashboards.
The Branch Manager Invariant Violated event fires from the reconciler job (not the observer) — the observer's SELECT ... FOR UPDATE should have prevented the invariant break. Every fire is a P1.
